SEARCH

Class BinarySearchTree<E extends Comparable<E>>

java.lang.Object
BinarySearchTree<E>
Type Parameters:
E - the type of elements stored in the tree
public class BinarySearchTree<E extends Comparable<E>> extends Object
A generic Binary Search Tree (BST) implementation that stores elements in sorted order using the Comparable interface.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    TreeNode<E>
    root
     

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    BinarySearchTree()
    Constructs an empty Binary Search Tree.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    add(E value)
    Adds a value to the binary search tree while maintaining BST ordering rules.
    boolean
    contains(E value)
    Checks whether a given value exists in the binary search tree.
    int
    countLeafNodes()
    Counts the number of leaf nodes (nodes with no children) in the tree.
    int
    countNodes()
    Returns the total number of nodes currently in the tree.
    E
    delete(E value)
    Deletes a value from the binary search tree while maintaining BST structure.
    int
    getHeight()
    Returns the height of the binary search tree.
    TreeNode<E>
    getRoot()
    Returns the root node of the binary search tree.
    void
    printInorder(TreeNode<E> node)
    Prints the tree using an inorder traversal (Left → Root → Right).
    void
    printPostorder(TreeNode<E> node)
    Prints the tree using a postorder traversal (Left → Right → Root).
    void
    printPreorder(TreeNode<E> node)
    Prints the tree using a preorder traversal (Root → Left → Right).
    void
    setRoot(TreeNode<E> root)
    Sets the root node of the binary search tree.

    • root

      public TreeNode<E extends Comparable<E>> root

  • Constructor Details

    • BinarySearchTree

      public BinarySearchTree()
      Constructs an empty Binary Search Tree.

  • Method Details

    • getRoot

      public TreeNode<E> getRoot()
      Returns the root node of the binary search tree.
      Returns:
      the root TreeNode of the tree

    • setRoot

      public void setRoot(TreeNode<E> root)
      Sets the root node of the binary search tree.
      Parameters:
      root - the new root node

    • add

      public void add(E value)
      Adds a value to the binary search tree while maintaining BST ordering rules.
      Parameters:
      value - the value to insert into the tree

    • contains

      public boolean contains(E value)
      Checks whether a given value exists in the binary search tree.
      Parameters:
      value - the value to search for
      Returns:
      true if the value exists, false otherwise

    • countNodes

      public int countNodes()
      Returns the total number of nodes currently in the tree.
      Returns:
      number of nodes

    • countLeafNodes

      public int countLeafNodes()
      Counts the number of leaf nodes (nodes with no children) in the tree.
      Returns:
      number of leaf nodes

    • getHeight

      public int getHeight()
      Returns the height of the binary search tree.
      Returns:
      height of the tree

    • printInorder

      public void printInorder(TreeNode<E> node)
      Prints the tree using an inorder traversal (Left → Root → Right).
      Parameters:
      node - the starting node for traversal

    • printPreorder

      public void printPreorder(TreeNode<E> node)
      Prints the tree using a preorder traversal (Root → Left → Right).
      Parameters:
      node - the starting node for traversal

    • printPostorder

      public void printPostorder(TreeNode<E> node)
      Prints the tree using a postorder traversal (Left → Right → Root).
      Parameters:
      node - the starting node for traversal

    • delete

      public E delete(E value)
      Deletes a value from the binary search tree while maintaining BST structure. Handles leaf nodes, nodes with one child, and nodes with two children.
      Parameters:
      value - the value to delete
      Returns:
      the deleted value, or null if not found
